_README: Golden Q&A set — the backbone of the chat-groundedness eval. Copy this file to golden_qa.jsonl and replace the examples with 30–50 real questions about YOUR resume + repos. Each row: id, question, answer (ground truth), source (the chunk id from corpus/chunks.jsonl that should be retrieved, or null for out-of-corpus), type. Include ~10 out-of-corpus questions whose correct answer is a refusal. Mix in commit-history-only questions to prove the persona reads commit history, not just READMEs. The judge (judge.py) scores each agent answer as grounded/fabricated/refused; retrieval_eval.py uses the 'source' field to compute precision/recall.

question: What did the candidate study and where?
What is the candidate's most recent role and what did they do there?
Which programming languages does the candidate know best?
What is the <repo-name> project and what tech stack does it use?
What design tradeoff did the candidate make in <repo-name>?
In <repo-name>, what changed in the commit history after the initial version — what feature was added later?
What was one of the earliest meaningful commits in <repo-name> about?
Why is the candidate a good fit for an AI engineer role?
What is the candidate's current salary?
Did the candidate work at Amazon?
answer: Not according to what I have — I should not claim that.
